queue.title: Auto-retire stuck queue items in A prehook
queue.description: Extend audit-stats.mjs age check to auto-retire pending items with created_session > 50 sessions behind current, instead of creating separate wq items to retire them (as happened with wq-969/wq-974). Eliminates this class of meta-task.

outcome.note: autoRetireStuckItems() in audit-stats.mjs auto-retires pending items >50 sessions old during A prehook. Moves to archive with retirement outcome. Integrated into a-prehook-runner.mjs (check 6) and 35-a-session-prehook_A.sh (check 8). End-to-end tested.

queue.title: session-snapshots.mjs: export functions for unit testing without subprocess
queue.description: (added ~s1979): The current test uses execSync subprocess calls because session-snapshots.mjs has no exports — all functions are file-local and the CLI runs at module scope. Refactoring to export `ecosystemSnapshot` and `patternSnapshot` with injectable fs deps (like e-session-seed.mjs pattern) would make tests faster (no subprocess overhead ~50ms/test), enable edge-case testing for individual fields, and align with the DI pattern used by other hooks/lib modules. Low risk refactor: add exports, guard CLI with `import.meta.url` check.

queue.title: Consolidate E prehook node subprocesses into single runner
queue.description: Apply the same runner pattern from wq-971 (a-prehook-runner.mjs) to 35-e-session-prehook_E.sh. The E prehook currently spawns 9 node subprocesses (seed, liveness, topic-clusters, conversation-balance, spending-policy, credential-health, variety-analyzer, colony-jwt, picker + revalidate). Audit flags it slow at 6292ms avg. A single e-prehook-runner.mjs that imports and runs the checks would eliminate ~1-2s of Node startup overhead. Requires: export functions from each module (some already exported), create runner, update shell script to call runner once. Same pattern as wq-971.

queue.title: Lint platform-circuits.json for missing status fields on high-failure entries
queue.description: When consecutive_failures >= 3 but status is not "closed", audit re-flags platforms for manual circuit-breaking. A validation script that lints all entries where consecutive_failures >= threshold and verifies status === "closed" would catch metadata drift automatically. Lightweight jq/node script.

queue.title: Auto-circuit-break E posthook integration test
queue.description: wq-978 wired auto-circuit-break into recordOutcome() in circuit-breaker.mjs. Validate the full end-to-end pipeline: simulate 3 consecutive E session failures for a platform and verify platform-circuits.json has status:closed without manual B session intervention. Also verify the auto-reopen-on-success path. Test should exercise the actual engage-orchestrator → circuit-breaker → platform-circuits.json flow.
